name: chronicle-assistant-guide
description: Project-agnostic guidance for AI assistants using Chronicle. Provides search-first directives, best practices, and workflow patterns across ALL Chronicle-tracked projects. Works with or without MCP server.
capabilities: Search past Chronicle sessions for context before implementing features
Use MCP tools for fast structured queries when available
Fall back to CLI commands when MCP server is unavailable
Check project roadmap and milestones before starting work
Debug issues by searching for similar past problems
Understand session relationships and project history
author: ChandlerHardy
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown guidance. No executable code, file access, network calls, or command execution capabilities. All 90 static findings are false positives triggered by pattern-matching on documentation text.
valueStatement: Chronicle tracks every AI session, creating a searchable history of your work. This guide provides essential directives for AI assistants to leverage past sessions, avoid repeated mistakes, and follow proven workflows.
limitations: Does not execute Chronicle commands - only provides guidance
Requires Chronicle to be installed and configured
MCP server must be running for fastest tool access
Cannot create or modify sessions - only reads existing data

useCases.title: Search before implementing
useCases.description: Search past sessions to avoid repeating mistakes and learn from previous solutions

useCases.title: Understand project history
useCases.description: Review session summaries to understand how features were implemented

useCases.title: Find similar past issues
useCases.description: Search for error messages to discover how similar problems were solved

promptTemplates.title: Get started with Chronicle
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I use Chronicle to search past sessions and understand project context?

promptTemplates.title: Search for context
promptTemplates.prompt: Search Chronicle for information about implementing authentication features

promptTemplates.title: Debug with history
promptTemplates.prompt: Search Chronicle for information about MCP server hangs or freezes

promptTemplates.title: View roadmap
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the current roadmap and milestones for this project

bestPractices: Always search Chronicle before implementing features or debugging issues
Use MCP tools for fast structured responses when available, fall back to CLI
Read session summaries to understand past approaches and avoid repeated mistakes
antiPatterns: Jumping straight into implementation without searching for related past work
Ignoring related sessions in search results
Forgetting to check the roadmap before creating new milestones

faq.question: What AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: Works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. MCP tools require Chronicle MCP server running.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance only. Chronicle must be installed separately to execute commands.

faq.question: How does MCP differ from CLI?
faq.answer: MCP provides fast structured JSON responses under 10ms. CLI uses subprocess calls around 100ms but works everywhere.

faq.question: Is my session data stored safely?
faq.answer: Chronicle stores sessions locally in ~/.ai-session/ directory. No data is sent to external servers.

faq.question: What if MCP server is not available?
faq.answer: All functionality works via CLI commands. Chronicle searches, roadmap, and sessions all support CLI fallback.

faq.question: How is this different from other skills?
faq.answer: This skill is project-agnostic and works across ALL Chronicle-tracked projects with universal directives.
